news.summary: Delta Air Lines, Inc. (NYSE: DAL) is gearing up to release its second-quarter earnings on July 10 amidst a challenging period. Analysts predict earnings of $2.03 per share for the quarter, a decrease from $2.36 per share last year. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $16.18 billion, down from $16.66 billion the previous year. Delta Air Lines, along with Korean Air, announced a joint investment of $550 million in WestJet, a Canadian carrier, on May 9. This move has garnered attention from investors looking to capitalize on dividends. The latest developments concerning Delta Air Lines can be found on financial news platforms.
news.headline: Investors eyeing $500 monthly gains from Delta Air Lines stock with Q2 earnings at $2.03 per share and $16.18 billion revenue.

news.headline: The Little Red Dog launches July fundraiser to combat rising euthanasia rates and dwindling donations in Orange County.

news.summary: Pickering Interfaces, the top provider of modular signal switching and simulation solutions for electronic test and verification, has revealed the enhancement of its 41-670 (PXI) and 43-670 (PXIe) LVDT, RVDT, and resolver simulator modules. These modules now support high-speed resolver simulation up to 130kRPM rotation. This development caters to the increasing demand for accurate testing of advanced servo systems in crucial sectors like automotive and aerospace.
news.headline: Pickering Interfaces launches high-speed PXI resolver simulation modules for aerospace and automotive applications with speeds up to 130kRPM.

news.summary: In Carlsbad, California, Thermo Fisher Scientific has shared that the US FDA has given the green light to the Oncomine™ Dx Express Test on the Ion Torrent™ Genexus™ Dx Integrated Sequencer. This approval allows the test to be utilized as an in vitro diagnostic (IVD) assay and as a companion diagnostic (CDx) for Dizal’s ZEGFROVY® (sunvozertinib) and in tumor profiling. With this development, rapid next-generation sequencing (NGS) is now a step closer to being available in decentralized clinical settings.
news.headline: FDA approves Thermo Fisher's NGS assay for ZEGFROVY companion diagnostic and tumor profiling, bringing rapid sequencing to clinical settings.

Beginning with the introduction of personal computers in households, followed by smartphones revolutionizing social interaction and information access, and further advancements such as the Internet of Things interconnecting devices. This technological progression has seen artificial intelligence becoming a pervasive element in various aspects of life. The impact of technology on society continues to grow as more devices become interconnected, with AI at the forefront of innovation and change.

news.summary: E2open Parent Holdings, Inc. will announce its first quarter earnings post-market on Thursday, July 10. Analysts anticipate the Texas-based firm to reveal earnings of $0.04 per share, matching the previous year's first quarter. Revenue for this quarter is expected to be $148.92 million, lower than the $151.16 million reported in the same period last year. Following a recent strategic assessment, E2open agreed on May 25 to be purchased by WiseTech Global at $3.30 per share in an all-cash transaction. The company's stock concluded Wednesday's trading session at $3.25, up by 0.6%. For further details, interested parties can access the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Top Wall Street forecasters adjust expectations for E2open ahead of Q1 earnings release. Analysts expect earnings at 4 cents per share and revenue at $148.92 million, down from $151.16 million a year earlier. E2open to be acquired by WiseTech Global for $3.30 per share.

news.summary: Anthony Scaramucci, founder and managing partner of SkyBridge Capital, stated in an interview that he believes the recent trend of public companies embracing crypto treasury strategies will not be sustainable. Scaramucci noted that the current trend of companies following Michael Saylor's Bitcoin treasury model is temporary and will likely diminish over time. He suggested that investors might soon realize they are better off buying crypto assets directly instead of paying a premium for shares in companies that predominantly hold Bitcoin or other cryptocurrencies. Several companies have adopted crypto treasury strategies over the past year, taking inspiration from Saylor's approach at MicroStrategy (now known as Strategy). One such company is BitMine Immersion Technologies, a Bitcoin mining firm listed on the AMEX under the ticker BMNR. Scaramucci questioned the rationale behind paying intermediaries when investors could access crypto assets themselves.
news.headline: Anthony Scaramucci predicts public companies' trend of adopting crypto treasury strategies, like Michael Saylor's Bitcoin model, will not last. Scaramucci believes this will fade as investors may opt to purchase crypto assets themselves, rather than pay a premium for company shares holding them. Several companies have followed the trend in the past year.

news.summary: In London on July 3, 2025, RedCloud Holdings plc, also known as RedCloud, disclosed a securities purchase deal with select institutional and accredited investors. news.headline: RedCloud secures $13.5M in private placement of securities with institutional investors, aims to raise funds.

news.summary: Yesterday, Landsbankinn held a covered bond auction where holders of the series LBANK CB 25 were given the opportunity to participate in a covered bond exchange offering. In this exchange, holders could sell their covered bonds in the series at a predefined clean price of 99.204 and acquire covered bonds purchased in the auction.
news.headline: Landsbankinn hf. held a covered bond exchange offering, allowing series LBANK CB 25 holders to sell bonds at a fixed price of 99.204.

news.summary: In Dover, Del., the company Precisio recently administered the first-ever dose of its staph lysin, ClyO (LYSG101), to a patient suffering from a prosthetic joint infection that had shown resistance to existing treatments. The FDA greenlit this intervention through a compassionate care IND application submitted by the Mayo Clinic. The patient demonstrated good tolerability to the treatment with no reported adverse effects. While it is premature to determine complete efficacy, this development marks a remarkable advancement in expanding treatment choices for patients in similar situations.
news.headline: Precisio dosed first patient with ClyO (Staph Lysin) for drug-resistant prosthetic joint infection, no adverse events noted.FDA-approved.

news.summary: HKW, a middle market private equity firm, has recently completed the acquisition of Electric Equipment & Engineering Co. (EEE). EEE is renowned for manufacturing top-tier electrical power products catering to the power, telecommunications, and broadband sectors. James Raymond, the CEO of EEE, noted that HKW's alignment with their commitment to delivering premium power solutions to clients makes them an ideal partner. The acquisition signifies a strategic move by HKW, known for their focus on growth companies, leveraging their expertise to support EEE's operations.
news.headline: Private equity firm HKW acquires Electric Equipment & Engineering Co., manufacturer of electrical power products, aiming for quality service, per CEO James Raymond.

news.headline: Pigenius launches new sunshade guide to help drivers beat the summer heat, focusing on car protection for 30 years.

news.summary: A contentious proposal by United Parcel Service (UPS) is set to be revealed this month, ignoring its responsibility to generate employment opportunities. The plan involves the buyout of full-time workers represented by the Teamsters union. This move has sparked concerns as it could potentially leave these employees without jobs and responsible for their own healthcare coverage. The details of this controversial plan will be disclosed later in July.
news.headline: UPS plans to violate Teamsters contract with buyouts, jeopardizing jobs and health care for workers.

news.summary: In a recent update, it was reported that approximately 300,000 people in the region were affected by the severe weather conditions, leading to 50 reported casualties and over 100 injuries. The devastating floods have damaged over 1,000 homes and displaced more than 10,000 residents. Relief efforts are currently underway, with emergency services working tirelessly to provide aid to those impacted. The local government has allocated 1 million dollars towards recovery efforts and has urged residents to prioritize their safety during this challenging time.
news.headline: Quadient wins award for excellent financial communication at 2025 Transparency Awards. #Recognition #Excellence

news.summary: In Dublin, a report titled "Tyre Industry in South Africa 2025" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The report delves into the South African tyre industry, a notable component of the automotive sector. It covers various aspects such as the production of new and retreaded tyres, imports, distribution, domestic sales to original equipment manufacturers, replacement tyres through retail outlets and fitment centers, export sales, and waste tyre management. This comprehensive analysis provides valuable insights into an industry that plays a crucial role in the country's economy.
news.headline: South Africa Tyre Industry Report 2025 features 17 notable players; focus on manufacture, new & retreaded tyres, and waste management - ResearchAndMarkets.com.

news.summary: In Charlotte, North Carolina, Krispy Kreme, Inc. (NASDAQ: DNUT) revealed that Raphael Duvivier, currently serving as the President, International of Krispy Kreme, will take over as the Chief Financial Officer starting from July 11, 2025. This move follows the departure of Jeremiah Ashukian, the previous CFO, who opted to leave the company for a new role in a private firm. Mr. Duvivier, who has been with Krispy Kreme since 2019, has undertaken various leadership positions within the organization, including serving as a segment Chief Fina.
news.headline: Krispy Kreme appoints Raphael Duvivier as CFO, effective July 11, 2025, replacing Jeremiah Ashukian. Duvivier served as President, International.
